Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Dustin M. Seesemann, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Herbert Wilson.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY in the amount of $474,500.00 in Economic Damages, $300,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $774,500.00.
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-02-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, Maintaining The Current Trial Date
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Herbert Wilson
Lori A. Cataldo of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / opposing party Campbell Industries
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Alexander J. Alpert of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendants Thomas Dee Engineering Company and CBS Corporation
Vivy D. Dang of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-397-9006), appearing for defendant ATKN Company
Jennifer Ways of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), specially appearing for defendant Trane US Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, MAINTAINING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to maintain the trial date with preference is granted. The December 5, 2016 trial date is maintained with preference status pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 17, 2017. All dates/deadlines related to discovery, motions and disclosures remain dictated by the relevant provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure, California Rules of Court and Local Rules, according to the December 5, 2016 trial date. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by November 28, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on December 1, 2016. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on December 5, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by December 1, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
